Checks

ACC-001: Privileged Users adminCount1 Fail

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 4 | Duration: 278 ms | Score: 16

Misconfiguration: user accounts with adminCount=1 (protected object flag); review for inappropriate persistence of admin protection.

Remediation: Remove inappropriate adminSDHolder protection; relocate accounts to correct OUs with least-privilege delegation; investigate unexpected adminCount=1 on non-privileged users.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-c--protected-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • Persistence via adminSDHolder (MITRE T1484.002)

ACC-002: Privileged Groups adminCount1 Fail

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 14 | Duration: 37 ms | Score: 40

Misconfiguration: security groups with adminCount=1; review membership and whether protection is expected.

Remediation: Validate each group is an intentional protected group; remove nested or unintended memberships; replace ad-hoc protected groups with delegated OU structure where possible.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-c--protected-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • Protected groups (Microsoft Docs)

ACC-003: Privileged Computers adminCount1 Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 17 ms | Score: 0

Misconfiguration: computer accounts with adminCount=1; validate each is an intentional protected object.

Remediation: Confirm Tier0/Tier1 systems only; remove adminCount=1 from non-DC servers that should not be adminSDHolder-protected; align with tiering model.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-c--protected-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • Persistence on domain infrastructure (MITRE T1098)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-004: Users with SIDHistory Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 23 ms | Score: 0

Exposure: user accounts with sIDHistory populated (migration or attack persistence); validate legitimacy.

Remediation: Remove stale sIDHistory values after migration cutover; investigate unexpected values; restrict who can write sIDHistory (e.g. domain admin only).

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/win32/ad/sid-history
  • SID-History injection (MITRE T1134.005)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-006: Computers with RBCD Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 34 ms | Score: 0

Misconfiguration: resource-based constrained delegation (RBCD) on computers; review msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity.

Remediation: Clear msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity where not required; scope principals to least privilege; monitor changes to RBCD attributes.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/credentials-protection-and-management/resource-based-constrained-delegation-overview
  • Resource-based constrained delegation abuse (MITRE T1558.001)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-007: Users with RBCD Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

Misconfiguration: RBCD on user accounts; review delegation to constrained targets.

Remediation: Remove RBCD from user objects unless required; prefer computer-based delegation patterns; audit who can modify these attributes.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/credentials-protection-and-management/resource-based-constrained-delegation-overview
  • Resource-based constrained delegation abuse (MITRE T1558.001)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-009: KeyCredentialLink on Computers Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 7 ms | Score: 0

Shadow credentials: msDS-KeyCredentialLink can allow takeover without knowing the current password (WHfB / NTLM-less abuse). Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Restrict who can write KeyCredentialLink; audit keys on Tier0; remove unauthorized key credentials.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-011: Trust Accounts for Delegation Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

Unconstrained delegation on a user — impersonation and credential theft across services. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation from user accounts unless strictly required.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-012: Users Can Create DNS Records Fail

Category: Access_Control | Severity: medium | Findings: 15 | Duration: 74 ms | Score: 30

Infrastructure: Users Can Create DNS Records — DNS, time, or core service misconfiguration. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Sync time; secure DNS; validate SRV records.

References:

  • Windows Server security baseline
  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/

ACC-013: GPO Folder Rights Review Fail

Category: Access_Control | Severity: medium | Findings: 2 | Duration: 18 ms | Score: 6

Group Policy: GPO Folder Rights Review — GPO or link may weaken security or enable persistence. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Review GPO scope and SYSVOL ACLs; enforce change control; remove weak policies.

References:

  • Group Policy (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1484.001 (Group Policy Modification)

ACC-025: Users Can Add Computers to Domain Fail

Category: Access_Control | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 57 ms | Score: 3

Domain ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota greater than 0 allows non-admin users to create computer accounts (NTLM relay / machine account abuse). Evaluated from LDAP attribute.

Remediation: Set ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota to 0 unless business-approved.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/
ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota distinguishedName name CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
10 DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local sevenkingdoms ACC-025 Users Can Add Computers to Domain 1 Fail medium Domain ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota greater than 0 allows non-admin users to create computer accounts (NTLM relay / machine account abuse). Evaluated from LDAP attribute.

ACC-034: Kerberoastable Accounts Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Misconfiguration: user accounts with SPNs (Kerberoastable); review SPNs and use group managed service accounts where possible.

Remediation: Remove unnecessary SPNs from user accounts; prefer gMSA for services; enforce strong password policy for remaining SPN owners.

References:

  • Kerberoasting (MITRE T1558.003)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-037: Shadow Credentials Detection Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 253 ms | Score: 0

Shadow credentials: msDS-KeyCredentialLink can allow takeover without knowing the current password (WHfB / NTLM-less abuse). Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Restrict who can write KeyCredentialLink; audit keys on Tier0; remove unauthorized key credentials.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-038: Constrained Delegation Targets Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 167 ms | Score: 0

Constrained delegation: service may impersonate users to specific SPNs — path to privilege escalation. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Minimize SPN targets; remove stale delegation; prefer RBCD for resource servers.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-039: Resource Based Constrained Delegation Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 14 ms | Score: 0

Resource-based constrained delegation (RBCD) — misconfiguration can allow impersonation to the resource. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Clear RBCD where not needed; scope principals; monitor changes to the attribute.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-043: Constrained Delegation Principals Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Constrained delegation: service may impersonate users to specific SPNs — path to privilege escalation. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Minimize SPN targets; remove stale delegation; prefer RBCD for resource servers.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

ACC-044: Admin SDHolder Template Pass

Category: Access_Control | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

Unconstrained delegation on a user — impersonation and credential theft across services. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation from user accounts unless strictly required.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

ACE-001: Dangerous ACE: GenericAll (non-privileged principal) Fail

Category: ACE | Severity: high | Findings: 2253 | Duration: 1492 ms | Score: 40

Objects where a non-baseline principal has Allow GenericAll (full control). Review delegation and group memberships.

Remediation: Remove unnecessary GenericAll; prefer more specific rights; validate nested groups and shadow admins.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/implementing-least-privilege-administrative-models

CN=WINTERFELL,CN=Computers,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local WINTERFELL winterfell.sevenkingdoms.local top NT AUTHORITY\SELF S-1-5-10 GenericAll ReadProperty, WriteProperty, ExtendedRight 91e647de-d96f-4b70-9557-d63ff4f3ccd8 Allow ACE-001 Dangerous ACE: GenericAll (non-privileged principal) 2253 Fail high Objects where a non-baseline principal has Allow GenericAll (full control). CN=WINTERFELL,CN=Computers,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local WINTERFELL winterfell.sevenkingdoms.local top BUILTIN\Pre-Windows 2000 Compatible Access S-1-5-32-554 GenericAll ListChildren Allow ACE-001 Dangerous ACE: GenericAll (non-privileged principal) 2253 Fail high Objects where a non-baseline principal has Allow GenericAll (full control). Review delegation and group memberships.

ACE-002: Dangerous ACE: GenericWrite (non-privileged principal) Fail

Category: ACE | Severity: high | Findings: 1047 | Duration: 740 ms | Score: 40

Objects where a non-baseline principal has Allow GenericWrite (broad write access). Often enables property takeover or lateral movement.

Remediation: Tighten ACLs; remove GenericWrite from low-trust principals; review OU and GPO delegation.

References:

  • https://posts.specterops.io/certified-pre-owned-ada1f94c90a2

Often enables property takeover or lateral movement.

ACE-003: Dangerous ACE: WriteDacl (non-privileged principal) Fail

Category: ACE | Severity: critical | Findings: 80 | Duration: 366 ms | Score: 50

Objects where a non-baseline principal can modify the DACL (grant self further rights).

Remediation: Restrict WriteDacl to Domain Admins / delegated PKI or GPO owners as appropriate; audit nested groups.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/win32/adschema/a-rights
DistinguishedName Name DnsHostName ObjectClass Principal PrincipalSID MatchedDangerousRights ActiveDirectoryRights ObjectType AccessControlType CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description

ACE-004: Dangerous ACE: WriteOwner (non-privileged principal) Fail

Category: ACE | Severity: critical | Findings: 79 | Duration: 305 ms | Score: 50

Objects where a non-baseline principal can take ownership (often leading to full control).

Remediation: Remove WriteOwner from untrusted principals; validate sensitive OUs and GPOs.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/win32/adschema/a-rights
DistinguishedName Name DnsHostName ObjectClass Principal PrincipalSID MatchedDangerousRights ActiveDirectoryRights ObjectType AccessControlType CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description

ACE-005: Broad ACE: WriteProperty (non-privileged principal) Fail

Category: ACE | Severity: info | Findings: 544 | Duration: 601 ms | Score: 10

Objects with Allow WriteProperty for non-baseline principals (noisy; includes targeted property writes). Review ObjectType when present.

Remediation: Narrow rights to required properties; prefer validated admin paths.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/win32/adschema/a-rights
DistinguishedName Name DnsHostName ObjectClass Principal PrincipalSID MatchedDangerousRights ActiveDirectoryRights ObjectType AccessControlType CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description

AD-005: KRBTGT Account Password Age Fail

Category: Security_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 3

Privileged access: KRBTGT Account Password Age — standing privilege or stale access. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: PIM; JIT; PAWS; minimal standing admin.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)
whenCreated objectClass whenChanged name distinguishedName samAccountName CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
03/05/2026 11:22:11 top 03/06/2026 02:48:57 krbtgt CN=krbtgt,CN=Users,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local krbtgt AD-005 KRBTGT Account Password Age 1 Fail medium Privileged access: KRBTGT Account Password Age — standing privilege or stale access. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted. C:\Users\acer\Music\AD_Security_Scripts_2026-02-05\Security_Accounts\AD-005_KRBTGT_Account_Password_Age\adsi.ps1

ADCS-ESC1: ESC1 Enrollee-supplied SAN (certificate template) Error

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 230 ms | Score: 0

Certificate templates with CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT, authentication-capable EKU, no manager approval, and non-privileged enrollment: arbitrary SAN / impersonation risk.

Remediation: Remove EnrolleeSuppliesSubject where not required; enable manager approval; restrict enrollment ACL to least privilege.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-template-permissions

Error: The property 'Count' cannot be found on this object. Verify that the property exists.

ADCS-ESC2: ESC2 Any Purpose or missing EKU (certificate template) Error

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 27 ms | Score: 0

Templates with Any Purpose EKU or no EKU, no manager approval, enrollable by non-privileged principals — master-key / CRA abuse risk.

Remediation: Restrict pKIExtendedKeyUsage; remove 2.5.29.37.0; enable manager approval.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)

Error: The property 'Count' cannot be found on this object. Verify that the property exists.

ADCS-ESC3: ESC3 Certificate Request Agent chain (templates) Error

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 35 ms | Score: 0

CRA template enrollable by non-privileged users and/or templates accepting CRA-signed requests — on-behalf enrollment / impersonation chain.

Remediation: Remove CRA EKU where not required; review msPKI-RA-Application-Policies; enable manager approval.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)

Error: The property 'Count' cannot be found on this object. Verify that the property exists.

ADCS-ESC4: ESC4 Weak template object ACLs Error

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 73 ms | Score: 0

Non-privileged principals with write/control on enabled certificate templates — can alter template flags (e.g. ESC1 conditions).

Remediation: Remove GenericWrite/WriteDacl/WriteOwner from non-admin principals on template objects.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-template-permissions

Error: The property 'Count' cannot be found on this object. Verify that the property exists.

ADCS-ESC5: ESC5 Weak ACLs on critical PKI containers Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: critical | Findings: 1 | Duration: 118 ms | Score: 5

Non-privileged write access to NTAuth, Enrollment Services, AIA, CDP, OID, or PKI roots — trust chain and policy manipulation risk.

Remediation: Restrict write to Enterprise Admins / designated PKI admins; audit object access to CN=Public Key Services.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
CheckId CheckName EscCheckId Title AdcsSeverity FindingDetail Remediation AffectedObjects Result Severity
ADCS-ESC5 ESC5 Weak ACLs on critical PKI containers ESC5 Non-admin write rights on PKI object: AIA Container Critical Object 'AIA Container' (CN=AIA,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local). ACEs: NT AUTHORITY\Authenticated Users [GenericRead] SEVENKINGDOMS\Cert Publishers [GenericAll] Remove non-admin write rights from critical PKI containers; enable auditing. CN=AIA,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local Fail critical

ADCS-ESC6: ESC6 CA EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 (policy module) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: critical | Findings: 1 | Duration: 165 ms | Score: 5

Enterprise CA policy may allow arbitrary SAN in requests (certutil EditFlags). Requires RPC access to CA or running on CA-adjacent host.

Remediation: Disable EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2; apply KB5014754; validate StrongCertificateBindingEnforcement.

References:

  • https://support.microsoft.com/topic/kb5014754
CheckId CheckName EscCheckId Title AdcsSeverity FindingDetail Remediation AffectedObjects Result Severity
ADCS-ESC6 ESC6 CA EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 (policy module) ESC6 ESC6 manual check: SEVENKINGDOMS-CA Info Could not read EditFlags for 'SEVENKINGDOMS-CA' (kingslanding.sevenkingdoms.local) via certutil. Run certutil -config 'kingslanding.sevenkingdoms.local\SEVENKINGDOMS-CA' -getreg policy\EditFlags on a host with CA access. SEVENKINGDOMS-CA Fail critical

ADCS-ESC7: ESC7 Weak ACLs on Enterprise CA object Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 1 | Duration: 24 ms | Score: 4

Non-privileged GenericWrite/WriteDacl on pKIEnrollmentService — maps to CA management abuse (ManageCA / officer workflows).

Remediation: Tighten CA object ACL in AD and certsrv.msc security; align with tier model.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/managing-permissions-on-the-enterprise-ca
CheckId CheckName EscCheckId Title AdcsSeverity FindingDetail Remediation AffectedObjects Result Severity
ADCS-ESC7 ESC7 Weak ACLs on Enterprise CA object ESC7 Non-admin write on CA object: SEVENKINGDOMS-CA High CA 'SEVENKINGDOMS-CA' (kingslanding.sevenkingdoms.local). Elevated AD permissions for non-admins. ACEs: NT AUTHORITY\Authenticated Users [GenericRead] SEVENKINGDOMS\KINGSLANDING$ [CreateChild, DeleteChild, Self, WriteProperty, DeleteTree, Delete, GenericRead, WriteDacl, WriteOwner] NT AUTHORITY\Authenticated Users [ExtendedRight] Restrict CA object permissions; review certsrv.msc Security tab. SEVENKINGDOMS-CA; kingslanding.sevenkingdoms.local Fail high

ADCS-ESC8: ESC8 Web Enrollment over HTTP / relay surface Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: critical | Findings: 1 | Duration: 5155 ms | Score: 5

HTTP Web Enrollment or NTLM-exposed certsrv increases relay risk; HTTPS-only still needs EPA verification.

Remediation: Require HTTPS; enable EPA on IIS certsrv; restrict NTLM; see KB5005413.

References:

  • https://support.microsoft.com/topic/kb5005413
CheckId CheckName EscCheckId Title AdcsSeverity FindingDetail Remediation AffectedObjects Result Severity
ADCS-ESC8 ESC8 Web Enrollment over HTTP / relay surface ESC8 Web Enrollment over HTTP: SEVENKINGDOMS-CA Critical CA 'SEVENKINGDOMS-CA' (kingslanding.sevenkingdoms.local). HTTP probe: http://kingslanding.sevenkingdoms.local/certsrv/ HTTPS probe: False Require HTTPS; enable EPA; disable Web Enrollment if unused (KB5005413). SEVENKINGDOMS-CA; http://kingslanding.sevenkingdoms.local/certsrv Fail critical

ADV-008: Machine Account Quota Abuse Fail

Category: Advanced_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 28 ms | Score: 3

Domain ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota greater than 0 allows non-admin users to create computer accounts (NTLM relay / machine account abuse). Evaluated from LDAP attribute.

Remediation: Set ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota to 0 unless business-approved.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/
ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota distinguishedName name CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
10 DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local sevenkingdoms ADV-008 Machine Account Quota Abuse 1 Fail medium Domain ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota greater than 0 allows non-admin users to create computer accounts (NTLM relay / machine account abuse). Evaluated from LDAP attribute.

ADV-009: ADCS Web Enrollment Vulnerabilities Pass

Category: Advanced_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

PKI (LDAP): ADCS Web Enrollment Vulnerabilities — template or ACL data may enable certificate-based abuse; review with PKI owners. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Review enrollment and template ACLs; disable weak or unused templates.

References:

  • AD CS / templates (LDAP view)
  • Microsoft PKI / AD CS hardening

No findings (Pass).

ADV-010: Domain Trust Authentication Vulnerabilities Fail

Category: Advanced_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 2 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 6

Trust or naming-context exposure: Domain Trust Authentication Vulnerabilities — review for weak or unnecessary trust paths. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate trust type and SID filtering; remove stale trusts; monitor changes.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/manage-security-best-practices/understanding-trust-relationships
  • MITRE T1482

AUTH-001: Accounts Without Kerberos Pre-Auth Pass

Category: Authentication | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

AS-REP roasting: Kerberos pre-authentication disabled — offline cracking of account passwords without a TGT. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Enable pre-authentication on accounts; use strong passwords; avoid DONT_REQ_PREAUTH except rare documented exceptions.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

AUTH-002: Accounts with Reversible Encryption Pass

Category: Authentication | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Authentication: Accounts with Reversible Encryption — weak policies or legacy authentication mechanisms. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Strong authentication; disable legacy protocols where possible.

References:

  • Password policy (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1110

No findings (Pass).

AUTH-003: Accounts with Password Not Required Fail

Category: Authentication | Severity: high | Findings: 2 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 8

PASSWD_NOT_REQD — account may allow blank or weak password. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Require passwords; clear PASSWD_NOT_REQD.

References:

  • Password policy (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1110

AUTH-004: Accounts with Never Expiring Password Fail

Category: Authentication | Severity: high | Findings: 13 | Duration: 21 ms | Score: 40

Password never expires — long-lived passwords increase reuse and offline cracking risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove on sensitive accounts; enforce rotation; use PSO exceptions sparingly.

References:

  • Password policy (Microsoft Docs)

Category: Authentication | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

Authentication: Locked Out Accounts — weak policies or legacy authentication mechanisms. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Strong authentication; disable legacy protocols where possible.

  • MITRE T1110

No findings (Pass).

AUTH-006: Accounts Requiring Password Change Pass

Category: Authentication | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Password-not-set or must-change patterns — stale or takeover-prone accounts. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Reset or disable unused accounts; enforce secure onboarding.

References:

  • Password policy (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1110

No findings (Pass).

Category: Authentication | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Authentication: Smart Card Required Accounts — weak policies or legacy authentication mechanisms. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

AUTH-008: Service Accounts SPN Set Pass

Category: Authentication | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove user SPNs where possible; use gMSA; enforce strong passwords for SPN owners.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

AUTH-009: Protected Users Group Members Pass

Category: Authentication | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Authentication: Protected Users Group Members — weak policies or legacy authentication mechanisms. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Strong authentication; disable legacy protocols where possible.

References:

  • Password policy (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1110

No findings (Pass).

AUTH-010: Accounts Using DES Encryption Pass

Category: Authentication | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Weak Kerberos encryption (DES / DES-capable) — tickets are easier to crack. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos domain-wide; clear USE_DES_KEY_ONLY on accounts; upgrade legacy apps.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

AUTH-018: Admin Accounts Without Delegation Protection Fail

Category: Authentication | Severity: high | Findings: 4 | Duration: 17 ms | Score: 16

adminCount=1 (adminSDHolder-protected object) — persistence of elevated ACLs or legacy protected state. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate protection is intended; remove inappropriate adminCount; align with tiering model.

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory

AUTH-031: Accounts with Never Expiring Passwords Fail

Category: Authentication | Severity: high | Findings: 13 | Duration: 26 ms | Score: 40

Password never expires — long-lived passwords increase reuse and offline cracking risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove on sensitive accounts; enforce rotation; use PSO exceptions sparingly.

References:

  • Password policy (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1110

AUTH-033: Privileged Accounts with Stale Passwords Fail

Category: Authentication | Severity: high | Findings: 4 | Duration: 13 ms | Score: 16

adminCount=1 (adminSDHolder-protected object) — persistence of elevated ACLs or legacy protected state. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate protection is intended; remove inappropriate adminCount; align with tiering model.

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory

BCK-003: Tombstone Lifetime Check Pass

Category: Backup_Recovery | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 21 ms | Score: 0

Tombstone lifetime below 90 days may reduce recovery window (organizational tuning).

Remediation: Align tombstoneLifetime with business recovery requirements.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/active-directory-forest-recovery-introduction

No findings (Pass).

BCK-004: Deleted Object Recovery Capability Pass

Category: Backup_Recovery | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

msDS-DeletedObjectLifetime below 180 days (example SLA) — tune compare value for your org.

Remediation: Set deleted object lifetime appropriate to recovery SLAs.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/get-started/adac/introduction-to-active-directory-administrative-center-enhancements--level-100-

No findings (Pass).

BCK-005: AD Recycle Bin Status Fail

Category: Backup_Recovery | Severity: high | Findings: 1 | Duration: 8 ms | Score: 4

AD Recycle Bin optional feature not present — deleted object restore unavailable.

Remediation: Enable AD Recycle Bin in forest (irreversible; plan change window).

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/get-started/adac/introduction-to-active-directory-administrative-center-enhancements--level-100-
Detail CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
No matching LDAP object; policy or feature may be absent (misconfiguration). BCK-005 AD Recycle Bin Status 1 Fail high AD Recycle Bin optional feature not present — deleted object restore unavailable.

CERT-001: Certificate templates (configuration partition listing) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 33 | Duration: 77 ms | Score: 10

Full LDAP listing of certificate template objects. Use for inventory and to scope ADCS-ESC reviews. Each row is a PKI object, not proof of vulnerability by itself.

Remediation: Use results for inventory and change tracking. Prioritize templates with client-auth EKU and weak enrollment ACLs using ADCS-ESC1–ESC8.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-templates

CERT-006: Templates Allowing Client Authentication Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 13 | Duration: 27 ms | Score: 30

Certificate templates that include client authentication EKU (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2). These are common targets for domain authentication abuse when combined with weak enrollment or subject/SAN settings.

Remediation: Restrict enrollment; require manager approval where appropriate; ensure subject/SAN flags and msPKI settings align with least privilege; cross-check ADCS-ESC1–ESC3.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-template-permissions
MsPKICertificateNameFlag Cn DistinguishedName Name PKIExtendedKeyUsage DisplayName CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

These are common targets for domain authentication abuse when combined with weak enrollment or subject/SAN settings. Certificate_Services/CERT-006_Templates_Allowing_Client_Authentication/adsi.ps1

CERT-007: Templates Allowing Smart Card Logon Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 4 | Duration: 9 ms | Score: 12

Templates granting smart card logon EKU (1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.2). Mis-issued smart card logon certs can support credential abuse paths; review issuance and physical controls.

Remediation: Limit enrollment; validate key archival and issuance policies; ensure smart card issuance aligns with tier model.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
Cn PKIExtendedKeyUsage Name DistinguishedName DisplayName CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

CERT-008: Templates Allowing PKINIT Client Auth Pass

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

Templates with PKINIT client authentication EKU (1.3.6.1.5.2.3.4). Certificates can be used in Kerberos PKINIT flows; combined with weak enrollment they raise domain authentication risk.

Remediation: Tighten enrollment ACLs; review PKINIT usage; validate KDC and domain controller certificate binding settings.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
  • PKINIT and certificate mapping (Microsoft Docs)

No findings (Pass).

CERT-009: Templates Allowing Code Signing Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 3

Templates allowing code signing EKU (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.3). Abuse can support malware signing or policy bypass if enrollment is too broad.

Remediation: Restrict code signing template enrollment to designated groups; audit issued certificates.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-templates
Cn PKIExtendedKeyUsage Name DistinguishedName DisplayName CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CodeSigning 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.3 CodeSigning CN=CodeSigning,CN=Certificate Templates,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local Code Signing CERT-009 Templates Allowing Code Signing 1 Fail medium Templates allowing code signing EKU (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.3). Abuse can support malware signing or policy bypass if enrollment is too broad. Certificate_Services/CERT-009_Templates_Allowing_Code_Signing/adsi.ps1

CERT-010: Templates Without Manager Approval Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 32 | Duration: 59 ms | Score: 40

Templates without manager approval (CT_FLAG_PEND_ALL_REQUESTS not set in msPKI-Enrollment-Flag). Speeds issuance and enlarges the window for mis-issued certificates when other controls fail.

Remediation: Enable manager approval for sensitive templates; combine with restricted enrollment and ADCS-ESC reviews.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)

CERT-011: Templates Without Authorized Signatures Required Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 32 | Duration: 50 ms | Score: 40

Templates with zero or missing required RA signatures (msPKI-RA-Signature). Weakens issuance workflow and can ease abuse when enrollment is misconfigured.

Remediation: Require RA signatures where policy demands; align with PKI officer workflows.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)

Weakens issuance workflow and can ease abuse when enrollment is misconfigured. Certificate_Services/CERT-011_Templates_Without_Authorized_Signatures_Required/adsi.ps1

CERT-012: Enterprise Certificate Authorities Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 1 | Duration: 11 ms | Score: 1

Enterprise CA enrollment service objects (pKIEnrollmentService) in AD. Lists published CAs and template bindings; use for mapping attack surface alongside ADCS-ESC6–ESC8.

Remediation: Validate CA host hardening, Web Enrollment, and RPC exposure; review certificateTemplates published per CA.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/ad-cs-topology
DistinguishedName Cn CACertificate Name DNSHostName CertificateTemplates CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

CERT-013: NTAuth Certificate Store Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 18 ms | Score: 3

NTAuthCertificates store in AD lists CAs trusted for domain authentication. Unexpected or stale entries can affect trust decisions.

Remediation: Keep NTAuth aligned with intentional enterprise roots; remove retired CAs after migration.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/distribute-certificates-to-clients
Cn Name DistinguishedName CACertificate CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

CERT-014: Trusted Root CAs in AD Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 3 | Duration: 13 ms | Score: 9

Certification authority objects under the enterprise PKI tree. Broad listing helps identify roots and intermediates published to the forest.

Remediation: Audit CA certificates for lifecycle and trust alignment; remove obsolete publishing.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)

CERT-015: Templates with No Expiration Validation Pass

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Templates missing pKIExpirationPeriod (or equivalent validity constraints visible in LDAP). May indicate legacy or inconsistent template definitions worth validating in CA policy.

Remediation: Define and enforce validity periods per template; validate on issuing CA.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-templates

No findings (Pass).

CERT-016: Templates Allowing Key Archival Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 7 | Duration: 16 ms | Score: 21

Templates with key archival enrollment flag set. Increases sensitivity: archived keys require strict access controls on KRA and CA database.

Remediation: Verify KRA configuration, ACLs, and auditing; limit templates with archival to required scenarios.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/key-archival

CERT-017: Templates with Exportable Private Key Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 9 | Duration: 18 ms | Score: 36

Templates allowing exportable private keys (msPKI-Private-Key-Flag). Eases credential theft if certificates are issued to interactive users or broadly enrolled.

Remediation: Disable exportable keys where not required; prefer hardware protection for high-value use cases.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
Cn Name DistinguishedName MsPKIPrivateKeyFlag DisplayName CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

CERT-018: Templates for Machine Certificates Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 4 | Duration: 13 ms | Score: 12

Templates with machine/enrollment name flags indicating machine certificate scenarios. Review for unintended domain computer authentication surface.

Remediation: Restrict enrollment to intended device OUs and groups; validate autoenrollment GPO scope.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-templates

CERT-019: Templates Allowing Renewal with Different Key Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: low | Findings: 19 | Duration: 34 ms | Score: 20

Templates allowing renewal with a new key pair. Can complicate revocation and lifecycle tracking if combined with weak identity binding.

Remediation: Ensure renewal policies and revocation workflows cover key-change renewals.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-templates

CERT-023: Accounts with Certificate Published Pass

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: low | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

User accounts with userCertificate populated. Published certs may support mapping or smart card scenarios; unexpected entries warrant review for stale or over-published credentials.

Remediation: Validate intentional publication; remove obsolete certificates from the directory.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/key-archival

No findings (Pass).

CERT-024: Computers with Certificate Published Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: low | Findings: 1 | Duration: 18 ms | Score: 2

Computer accounts with userCertificate populated. May reflect domain controller or service certs published to AD; unexpected hosts warrant validation.

Remediation: Confirm published certs match intended roles; rotate and clean up stale entries.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/distribute-certificates-to-clients

CERT-025: CRL Distribution Points Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 1 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 1

CRL distribution point objects in the configuration partition. Use to map revocation publication points and HTTP/S surface.

Remediation: Ensure CDP URLs are reachable and HTTPS where required; monitor expiration and replication.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-revocation
DistinguishedName Cn Name CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CN=SEVENKINGDOMS-CA,CN=kingslanding,CN=CDP,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local SEVENKINGDOMS-CA SEVENKINGDOMS-CA CERT-025 CRL Distribution Points 1 Fail info CRL distribution point objects in the configuration partition. Use to map revocation publication points and HTTP/S surface. Certificate_Services/CERT-025_CRL_Distribution_Points/adsi.ps1

CERT-026: [DACL review] Certificate templates (nTSecurityDescriptor for enrollment ACL analysis) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 33 | Duration: 161 ms | Score: 30

Lists templates with security descriptor loaded. LDAP cannot resolve 'Everyone' enrollment in one filter—manually review DACL for Authenticated Users / Domain Users enrollment. Prefer ADCS-ESC4 for write-equivalent abuse on templates.

Remediation: Audit enrollment ACEs on each template; remove broad enrollment where inappropriate; correlate with ADCS-ESC4.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-template-permissions
Cn Name DistinguishedName DisplayName NTSecurityDescriptor CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

CERT-027: Offline Root CA References Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 3 | Duration: 20 ms | Score: 3

CA objects referencing offline roots or cross-boundary trust material. Use for PKI topology documentation and continuity planning.

Remediation: Validate CRL/AIA publication for offline roots; document trust chains.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/ad-cs-topology

CERT-028: AIA Authority Information Access Objects Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 1 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 1

AIA container under Public Key Services. Authority Information Access publication affects chain building for clients.

Remediation: Ensure AIA URLs are correct, reachable, and aligned with issuance hierarchy.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/authority-information-access
DistinguishedName Cn Name CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CN=AIA,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local AIA AIA CERT-028 AIA Authority Information Access Objects 1 Fail info AIA container under Public Key Services. Authority Information Access publication affects chain building for clients. Certificate_Services/CERT-028_AIA_Authority_Information_Access_Objects/adsi.ps1

CERT-029: Templates with Subject Name from AD Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 10 | Duration: 12 ms | Score: 30

Templates with subject name from Active Directory (CT_FLAG_SUBJECT_ALT_REQUIRE_UPN or related name flags in msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag). Affects how subjects are bound to directory identities.

Remediation: Validate name flags against intended use; cross-check with ESC1-style SAN issues.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)

CERT-030: [Listing] Template schema version fields (all templates) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 33 | Duration: 33 ms | Score: 10

Lists all templates with schema/minor revision attributes. Useful for spotting legacy versions; not a vulnerability by itself.

Remediation: Deprecate legacy templates; migrate to supported schema versions with explicit EKU and ACLs.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-templates

CERT-031: [Listing] Certificate templates (full forest set; manual ESC review) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 33 | Duration: 23 ms | Score: 10

Broad listing only—same objects as a full template inventory. Use to drive manual review; does not imply vulnerable configuration without ADCS-ESC and ACL analysis.

Remediation: Cross-reference with ADCS-ESC1–ESC8 and per-template ACL review; retire unused templates.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)

CERT-032: [Listing] Templates for enrollment rights review (full set) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 33 | Duration: 37 ms | Score: 10

Lists every template with timestamps—LDAP does not enumerate enrollment ACEs in this filter. Use for workflow tracking; validate enrollment with ADCS-ESC4 and DACL review.

Remediation: Verify only intended groups may enroll; remove excessive ACEs.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-template-permissions

CERT-033: Client authentication templates (same LDAP scope as CERT-006) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 13 | Duration: 19 ms | Score: 10

Intentionally the same filter as CERT-006 (client authentication EKU). Retained for checklist parity; findings overlap—do not double-count risk. Scoring suppressed.

Remediation: Use CERT-006 or ADCS-ESC checks for reporting; disable one ID in overrides if duplicates are undesirable.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)

CERT-034: Certificate Authority Health Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 1 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 1

Enterprise CA objects with timestamps for health-style review. LDAP alone does not prove outage; use with monitoring and certutil/CA logs.

Remediation: Monitor CA services, CRL validity, and disk space on CA hosts.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/monitoring-and-logging
DistinguishedName WhenChanged Name WhenCreated CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

LDAP alone does not prove outage; use with monitoring and certutil/CA logs. Certificate_Services/CERT-034_Certificate_Authority_Health/adsi.ps1

CERT-035: CRL Distribution Point Availability Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 1 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 1

CRL distribution points with change metadata. Supports tracking revocation publication changes.

Remediation: Validate CRL publication schedules and HTTP/S endpoints.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-revocation

CERT-036: OCSP Responder Status Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 1 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 1

Enterprise CA objects relevant to OCSP deployment review. LDAP does not prove OCSP responder health—follow with endpoint tests.

Remediation: Deploy and monitor OCSP responders per Microsoft guidance; align AIA/OCSP URLs.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/ocsp-responder
DistinguishedName WhenChanged Name WhenCreated CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

CERT-037: [Listing] Templates for version drift review (full set) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 33 | Duration: 24 ms | Score: 10

Lists all templates—LDAP does not detect 'mismatch' without comparing to published CA policy. Manual review only.

Remediation: Consolidate templates; disable superseded definitions.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-templates

CERT-038: [Listing] Templates for autoenrollment policy alignment (full set) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 33 | Duration: 26 ms | Score: 10

Broad template listing for GPO/autoenrollment correlation. LDAP alone does not read autoenrollment GPOs.

Remediation: Scope autoenrollment GPOs tightly; review templates granted autoenrollment.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/autoenrollment

CERT-039: Templates with Certificate Request Agent EKU Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 4 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 16

Templates publishing the CRA EKU (1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.1). Overlaps ADCS-ESC3—validate enrollment and manager approval.

Remediation: Remove CRA EKU where unnecessary; enforce manager approval and enrollment restrictions.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
Cn PKIExtendedKeyUsage Name DistinguishedName DisplayName CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

Overlaps ADCS-ESC3—validate enrollment and manager approval. Certificate_Services/CERT-039_Certificate_Request_Agent_Abuse/adsi.ps1

Overlaps ADCS-ESC3—validate enrollment and manager approval. Certificate_Services/CERT-039_Certificate_Request_Agent_Abuse/adsi.ps1

Overlaps ADCS-ESC3—validate enrollment and manager approval. Certificate_Services/CERT-039_Certificate_Request_Agent_Abuse/adsi.ps1

CERT-040: Web Enrollment Security Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 3

Enterprise CA objects tied to Web Enrollment attack surface. Correlates with ADCS-ESC8 for HTTP/NTLM exposure—this LDAP check does not replace network verification.

Remediation: Require HTTPS, harden IIS certsrv, restrict NTLM; see KB5005413.

References:

  • https://support.microsoft.com/topic/kb5005413
DistinguishedName WhenChanged Name WhenCreated CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CN=SEVENKINGDOMS-CA,CN=Enrollment Services,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local 03/06/2026 01:54:05 SEVENKINGDOMS-CA 03/06/2026 01:54:05 CERT-040 Web Enrollment Security 1 Fail medium Enterprise CA objects tied to Web Enrollment attack surface. Correlates with ADCS-ESC8 for HTTP/NTLM exposure—this LDAP check does not replace network verification. Certificate_Services/CERT-040_Web_Enrollment_Security/adsi.ps1

CERT-041: Templates with supersession reference (msPKI-Template-Supersede) Pass

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

Templates that reference another template in the supersession chain. Validate whether superseded templates are disabled where policy requires.

Remediation: Disable superseded templates after migration; enforce supersession links.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-templates

No findings (Pass).

CERT-042: CA Certificate Expiration Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 3

Enterprise CAs for CA certificate lifetime review. Expired or soon-to-expire CA certs break chain validation.

Remediation: Plan CA cert renewal; monitor AIA/CRL publication during rollover.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/renew-a-certification-authority
DistinguishedName WhenChanged Name WhenCreated CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CN=SEVENKINGDOMS-CA,CN=Enrollment Services,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local 03/06/2026 01:54:05 SEVENKINGDOMS-CA 03/06/2026 01:54:05 CERT-042 CA Certificate Expiration 1 Fail medium Enterprise CAs for CA certificate lifetime review. Expired or soon-to-expire CA certs break chain validation. Certificate_Services/CERT-042_CA_Certificate_Expiration/adsi.ps1

CERT-043: Templates with minimal key size at or below 1024 bits (LDAP) Pass

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Templates advertising msPKI-Minimal-Key-Size ≤ 1024. Modern baselines typically require ≥2048; confirm algorithm context on issuing CA.

Remediation: Raise minimum key size on templates and CAs; reissue where non-compliant.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/cryptography

No findings (Pass).

CERT-044: [Listing] Template objects for validity period review (LDAP cannot compare periods) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 33 | Duration: 29 ms | Score: 10

Broad listing: LDAP does not decode pKIExpirationPeriod for numeric comparison in this check. Manual or CA-policy review required for 'excessive' validity.

Remediation: Reduce validity periods to operational minimums; enforce renewal discipline.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-templates

CERT-045: CA Audit Settings Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: low | Findings: 1 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 2

Enterprise CAs for audit configuration review. LDAP listing does not read local CA audit policy—use CA host baselines.

Remediation: Enable and forward CA audit logs; align with SIEM coverage.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/monitoring-and-logging
DistinguishedName WhenChanged Name WhenCreated CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CN=SEVENKINGDOMS-CA,CN=Enrollment Services,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local 03/06/2026 01:54:05 SEVENKINGDOMS-CA 03/06/2026 01:54:05 CERT-045 CA Audit Settings 1 Fail low Enterprise CAs for audit configuration review. LDAP listing does not read local CA audit policy—use CA host baselines. Certificate_Services/CERT-045_CA_Audit_Settings/adsi.ps1

CERT-046: [DACL review] Templates with security descriptor (ACL triage) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 33 | Duration: 34 ms | Score: 30

Loads nTSecurityDescriptor for manual or downstream ACL review. Prefer ADCS-ESC4 for automated weak-ACL detection.

Remediation: Remove write-equivalent rights for non-PKI admins; validate ADCS-ESC4 output.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-template-permissions

CERT-047: CA Database Backup Status Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 1 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 1

Enterprise CAs for backup posture review. AD LDAP does not prove backup success—follow with backup reports.

Remediation: Back up CA database and keys per Microsoft DR guidance; test restore.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/back-up-and-restore
DistinguishedName WhenChanged Name WhenCreated CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CN=SEVENKINGDOMS-CA,CN=Enrollment Services,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local 03/06/2026 01:54:05 SEVENKINGDOMS-CA 03/06/2026 01:54:05 CERT-047 CA Database Backup Status 1 Fail info Enterprise CAs for backup posture review. AD LDAP does not prove backup success—follow with backup reports. Certificate_Services/CERT-047_CA_Database_Backup_Status/adsi.ps1

CERT-048: Certificate Revocation Reasons Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 1 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 1

Enterprise CAs for revocation workflow documentation. Pair with operational CRL/OCSP monitoring.

Remediation: Maintain revocation reason policies and responder availability.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/certificate-revocation
DistinguishedName WhenChanged Name WhenCreated CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CN=SEVENKINGDOMS-CA,CN=Enrollment Services,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local 03/06/2026 01:54:05 SEVENKINGDOMS-CA 03/06/2026 01:54:05 CERT-048 Certificate Revocation Reasons 1 Fail info Enterprise CAs for revocation workflow documentation. Pair with operational CRL/OCSP monitoring. Certificate_Services/CERT-048_Certificate_Revocation_Reasons/adsi.ps1

CERT-049: [Listing] Smart card logon template review (full set; see CERT-007 for EKU-specific) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 33 | Duration: 26 ms | Score: 10

Legacy stub used a broad template list. For EKU-specific smart card templates use CERT-007. This row is listing-oriented; scoring suppressed.

Remediation: Validate smart card templates, KDC certs, and user mappings.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/smart-card-logon

CERT-050: Templates with Any Purpose or missing EKU (ESC2-style LDAP signal) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 3 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 12

Templates with Any Purpose EKU or no EKU—high-risk configuration class. Overlaps ADCS-ESC2; use ESC2 for full logic.

Remediation: Explicitly set required EKUs; remove Any Purpose where not justified.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)
Cn PKIExtendedKeyUsage Name DistinguishedName DisplayName CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

Overlaps ADCS-ESC2; use ESC2 for full logic. Certificate_Services/CERT-050_Certificate_Template_EKU_Misconfigurations/adsi.ps1
SubCA N/A SubCA CN=SubCA,CN=Certificate Templates,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local Subordinate Certification Authority CERT-050 Templates with Any Purpose or missing EKU (ESC2-style LDAP signal) 3 Fail high Templates with Any Purpose EKU or no EKU—high-risk configuration class. Overlaps ADCS-ESC2; use ESC2 for full logic. Certificate_Services/CERT-050_Certificate_Template_EKU_Misconfigurations/adsi.ps1

Overlaps ADCS-ESC2; use ESC2 for full logic. Certificate_Services/CERT-050_Certificate_Template_EKU_Misconfigurations/adsi.ps1

CERT-051: CA Role Separation Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 3

Enterprise CAs for CA role separation review. Combined CA admin + backup operator on same accounts increases insider risk.

Remediation: Separate CA administration per Microsoft PKI best practices.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/managing-permissions-on-the-enterprise-ca
DistinguishedName WhenChanged Name WhenCreated CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CN=SEVENKINGDOMS-CA,CN=Enrollment Services,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local 03/06/2026 01:54:05 SEVENKINGDOMS-CA 03/06/2026 01:54:05 CERT-051 CA Role Separation 1 Fail medium Enterprise CAs for CA role separation review. Combined CA admin + backup operator on same accounts increases insider risk. Certificate_Services/CERT-051_CA_Role_Separation/adsi.ps1

CERT-052: [Listing] Subject name policy review (all templates) Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: info | Findings: 33 | Duration: 28 ms | Score: 10

Broad listing for subject/SAN flag review. Prefer targeted checks (e.g. CERT-029, ADCS-ESC1) for specific misconfigurations.

Remediation: Tighten subject name flags; disable enrollee-supplied subject where not needed.

References:

  • SpecterOps Certified Pre-Owned (AD CS)

CERT-053: CA Security Descriptor Fail

Category: Certificate_Services | Severity: high | Findings: 1 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 4

Enterprise CA objects for security descriptor review. Weak ACLs on pKIEnrollmentService relate to ADCS-ESC7—use LDAP plus ESC7 for full analysis.

Remediation: Restrict ManageCA and related rights to PKI admins; validate with ADCS-ESC7.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-cs/manage/managing-permissions-on-the-enterprise-ca
DistinguishedName WhenChanged Name WhenCreated CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath
CN=SEVENKINGDOMS-CA,CN=Enrollment Services,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local 03/06/2026 01:54:05 SEVENKINGDOMS-CA 03/06/2026 01:54:05 CERT-053 CA Security Descriptor 1 Fail high Enterprise CA objects for security descriptor review. Weak ACLs on pKIEnrollmentService relate to ADCS-ESC7—use LDAP plus ESC7 for full analysis. Certificate_Services/CERT-053_CA_Security_Descriptor/adsi.ps1

CMGMT-007: Computers with Weak Encryption Pass

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

AS-REP roasting: Kerberos pre-authentication disabled — offline cracking of account passwords without a TGT. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Enable pre-authentication on accounts; use strong passwords; avoid DONT_REQ_PREAUTH except rare documented exceptions.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

CMGMT-008: Computers with Pre Windows 2000 Access Pass

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

Computer/server: Computers with Pre Windows 2000 Access — patching, local admin, or tiering issues. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Security baselines; reduce local admin sprawl; tier separation.

  • MITRE ATT&CK — Enterprise (Windows)

No findings (Pass).

CMGMT-009: Computers with Unconstrained Delegation Fail

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: critical | Findings: 1 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 5

Unconstrained delegation on a non-DC computer — TGT theft and lateral movement to any service. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove TRUSTED_FOR_DELEGATION from member servers; use constrained or RBCD; isolate Tier0-adjacent hosts.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)
whenCreated userAccountControl objectClass whenChanged name distinguishedName CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description SourcePath

CMGMT-010: Computers with Constrained Delegation Pass

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Constrained delegation: service may impersonate users to specific SPNs — path to privilege escalation. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Minimize SPN targets; remove stale delegation; prefer RBCD for resource servers.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

CMGMT-011: Computers with Resource Based Constrained Delegation Pass

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Resource-based constrained delegation (RBCD) — misconfiguration can allow impersonation to the resource. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Clear RBCD where not needed; scope principals; monitor changes to the attribute.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

CMGMT-014: Computers with AdminSDHolder Protection Pass

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

adminCount=1 (adminSDHolder-protected object) — persistence of elevated ACLs or legacy protected state. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate protection is intended; remove inappropriate adminCount; align with tiering model.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

CMGMT-018: Computers with Password Never Expires Pass

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Password never expires — long-lived passwords increase reuse and offline cracking risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove on sensitive accounts; enforce rotation; use PSO exceptions sparingly.

References:

  • Password policy (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1110

No findings (Pass).

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

  • MITRE ATT&CK — Enterprise (Windows)

No findings (Pass).

CMGMT-020: Computers with DES Encryption Only Pass

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Weak Kerberos encryption (DES / DES-capable) — tickets are easier to crack. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos domain-wide; clear USE_DES_KEY_ONLY on accounts; upgrade legacy apps.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

CMGMT-027: Computers with SID History Pass

Category: Computer_Management | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

SID History populated — migration artifact or malicious SID injection for privilege persistence. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove stale sIDHistory; investigate unexpected values; restrict writes to sIDHistory.

No findings (Pass).

CMP-002: Computers with Constrained Delegation Pass

Category: Computers_Servers | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 2 ms | Score: 0

Constrained delegation: service may impersonate users to specific SPNs — path to privilege escalation. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Minimize SPN targets; remove stale delegation; prefer RBCD for resource servers.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

CMP-003: Computers with RBCD Configured Pass

Category: Computers_Servers | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 2 ms | Score: 0

Resource-based constrained delegation (RBCD) — misconfiguration can allow impersonation to the resource. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Clear RBCD where not needed; scope principals; monitor changes to the attribute.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

CMP-008: Computers with adminCount1 Pass

Category: Computers_Servers | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

adminCount=1 (adminSDHolder-protected object) — persistence of elevated ACLs or legacy protected state. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate protection is intended; remove inappropriate adminCount; align with tiering model.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

CMP-009: Computers with SIDHistory Pass

Category: Computers_Servers | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

SID History populated — migration artifact or malicious SID injection for privilege persistence. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove stale sIDHistory; investigate unexpected values; restrict writes to sIDHistory.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

CMP-010: Computers with KeyCredentialLink Pass

Category: Computers_Servers | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Shadow credentials: msDS-KeyCredentialLink can allow takeover without knowing the current password (WHfB / NTLM-less abuse). Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Restrict who can write KeyCredentialLink; audit keys on Tier0; remove unauthorized key credentials.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

CMP-013: Computers with S4U Delegation Pass

Category: Computers_Servers | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 2 ms | Score: 0

Protocol transition / S4U (TRUSTED_TO_AUTH_FOR_DELEGATION) — constrained delegation abuse (S4U2Self/S4U2Proxy). Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Restrict msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo; remove unnecessary protocol transition; audit delegation.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

CMP-014: Computers with DES-Only Kerberos Pass

Category: Computers_Servers | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Weak Kerberos encryption (DES / DES-capable) — tickets are easier to crack. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos domain-wide; clear USE_DES_KEY_ONLY on accounts; upgrade legacy apps.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

CIS Level 1 style password checks (see Violations).

Remediation: Align with CIS Microsoft Windows Server benchmarks.

References:

  • CIS Benchmarks
Violations CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
minPwdLength 5 below minimum 14. COMPLY-002 CIS Benchmark Account Policies 1 Fail medium CIS Level 1 style password checks (see Violations).

COMPLY-003: PCI DSS Fail

Category: Compliance | Severity: medium | Findings: 2 | Duration: 10 ms | Score: 6

Example PCI-DSS-oriented password attribute review (see Violations).

Remediation: Align with PCI-DSS v4 password requirements.

References:

  • PCI SSC
Violations CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
minPwdLength 5 below minimum 12. lockoutThreshold 5 below minimum 6. COMPLY-003 PCI DSS 2 Fail medium Example PCI-DSS-oriented password attribute review (see Violations).

  • HIPAA Security Rule

minPwdLength 5 below minimum 8. COMPLY-004 HIPAA Controls 1 Fail medium Example HIPAA-oriented password checks (organizational tuning required).

Category: Compliance | Severity: medium | Findings: 130 | Duration: 96 ms | Score: 30

Remediation: Remediate to baseline; document waivers.

adminCount=1 (adminSDHolder-protected object) — persistence of elevated ACLs or legacy protected state. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate protection is intended; remove inappropriate adminCount; align with tiering model.

DC-003-dup2: DCs with Constrained Delegation Pass

Category: Domain_Controllers | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 2 ms | Score: 0

Constrained delegation: service may impersonate users to specific SPNs — path to privilege escalation. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Minimize SPN targets; remove stale delegation; prefer RBCD for resource servers.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

DC-033: DCs with SMB Signing Disabled (SYSVOL GPO) Pass

Category: Domain_Controllers | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 37 ms | Score: 0

Scans Group Policy SecEdit templates on SYSVOL for SMB server packet signing. Does not use remote registry; requires read access to \\domain\\SYSVOL like any domain-joined client.

Remediation: Set SMB server signing to required via GPO (or remove weak values); validate with security baseline.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/storage/file-server/smb-security
  • MITRE T1021.002

No findings (Pass).

DC-034: DCs LDAP Signing Not Required (SYSVOL GPO) Fail

Category: Domain_Controllers | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 38 ms | Score: 3

Detects weak LDAP signing policy pushed via GPO registry values in GptTmpl.inf (not a live DC registry probe).

Remediation: Require LDAP signing on domain controllers; set LDAPServerIntegrity to required in GPO.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-a--sites
  • MITRE T1040
RuleId GptTmplPath RegistryKeyLine RawValue ParsedDword Detail CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
NTDS_LDAPServerIntegrity \\sevenkingdoms.local\SYSVOL\sevenkingdoms.local\Policies\{6AC1786C-016F-11D2-945F-00C04fB984F9}\Machine\Microsoft\Windows NT\SecEdit\GptTmpl.inf MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters\LDAPServerIntegrity 4,1 1 GPO sets LDAPServerIntegrity to 0/1 (none or negotiate). Value 2 requires signing. DC-034 DCs LDAP Signing Not Required (SYSVOL GPO) 1 Fail medium Detects weak LDAP signing policy pushed via GPO registry values in GptTmpl.inf (not a live DC registry probe).

DC-035: DCs LDAP Channel Binding Disabled (SYSVOL GPO) Pass

Category: Domain_Controllers | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 41 ms | Score: 0

Reads ldapEnforceChannelBinding from SYSVOL GptTmpl.inf when present. Matches either NTDS or LSA policy paths under [Registry Values].

Remediation: Enable LDAP channel binding (EPA) per Microsoft guidance for DCs and clients.

References:

  • https://support.microsoft.com/help/4034879

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-001: Kerberos Encryption Types Domain Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Finding when RC4 (0x4) remains enabled in msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes; prefer AES-only Kerberos where supported.

Remediation: Disable RC4 for Kerberos where compatible with legacy apps.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-encryption-types

No findings (Pass).
