Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Domain or forest configuration: Recycle Bin Enabled Check — domain-wide security impact. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: AD security baseline; protect Tier0; validate FSMO and functional levels.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-003: ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota Setting Fail

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 3

Domain ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota greater than 0 allows non-admin users to create computer accounts (NTLM relay / machine account abuse). Evaluated from LDAP attribute.

Remediation: Set ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota to 0 unless business-approved.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/
ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota distinguishedName name CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
10 DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local sevenkingdoms DCONF-003 ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota Setting 1 Fail medium Domain ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota greater than 0 allows non-admin users to create computer accounts (NTLM relay / machine account abuse). Evaluated from LDAP attribute.

DCONF-004: Default Domain Password Policy Fail

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 8 ms | Score: 3

Default domain policy: finding when minPwdLength is below 14 (adjust threshold in catalog if needed).

Remediation: Harden default domain password policy per organizational baseline.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/
pwdProperties pwdHistoryLength minPwdAge name distinguishedName lockoutThreshold minPwdLength maxPwdAge CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
0 24 -864000000000 sevenkingdoms DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local 5 5 -32141664000000000 DCONF-004 Default Domain Password Policy 1 Fail medium Default domain policy: finding when minPwdLength is below 14 (adjust threshold in catalog if needed).

DCONF-005: Tombstone Lifetime Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Infrastructure: Tombstone Lifetime — DNS, time, or core service misconfiguration. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Sync time; secure DNS; validate SRV records.

References:

  • Windows Server security baseline
  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-006: Anonymous LDAP Access Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

LDAP: Anonymous LDAP Access — unsigned or cleartext LDAP risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Require LDAP signing and TLS; disable simple bind.

References:

  • LDAP signing (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1040

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-009: LDAP Signing Requirements (SYSVOL GPO) Fail

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 33 ms | Score: 3

Domain-wide LDAP signing posture via GPO SecEdit templates on SYSVOL.

Remediation: Require LDAP signing for domain controllers.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-a--sites
RuleId GptTmplPath RegistryKeyLine RawValue ParsedDword Detail CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
NTDS_LDAPServerIntegrity \\sevenkingdoms.local\SYSVOL\sevenkingdoms.local\Policies\{6AC1786C-016F-11D2-945F-00C04fB984F9}\Machine\Microsoft\Windows NT\SecEdit\GptTmpl.inf MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters\LDAPServerIntegrity 4,1 1 GPO sets LDAPServerIntegrity to 0/1 (not require signing). DCONF-009 LDAP Signing Requirements (SYSVOL GPO) 1 Fail medium Domain-wide LDAP signing posture via GPO SecEdit templates on SYSVOL.

DCONF-010: LDAP Channel Binding (SYSVOL GPO) Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 30 ms | Score: 0

SYSVOL-based detection of ldapEnforceChannelBinding=0 in GptTmpl.inf.

Remediation: Enable channel binding enforcement for LDAP over TLS.

References:

  • https://support.microsoft.com/help/4034879

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-011: SMB Signing Configuration (SYSVOL GPO) Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 28 ms | Score: 0

Evaluates SMB signing-related registry values as deployed via GPO SecEdit (SYSVOL), not live registry on endpoints.

Remediation: Require SMB signing on clients and servers per baseline.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/storage/file-server/smb-security

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-012: Null Session Access (SYSVOL GPO) Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 29 ms | Score: 0

Reads RestrictAnonymous from SYSVOL SecEdit GptTmpl.inf.

Remediation: Set RestrictAnonymous=2 via security policy.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/credentials-protection-and-management/

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-013: LM Hash Storage (SYSVOL GPO) Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 30 ms | Score: 0

SYSVOL policy for NoLMHash.

Remediation: Enable NoLMHash via GPO.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/credentials-protection-and-management/

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-014: NTLM Authentication Settings (SYSVOL GPO) Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 30 ms | Score: 0

Reads LmCompatibilityLevel from SYSVOL GptTmpl.inf [Registry Values] when administrators deploy it via security templates.

Remediation: Set LAN Manager authentication level to send NTLMv2 responses only (typically 3-5).

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/credentials-protection-and-management/understanding-lm-ntlm-ntlmv2-authentication

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-015: Kerberos ticket lifetime (LDAP placeholder — see ticket policy doc) Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: info | Findings: 0 | Duration: 15 ms | Score: 0

Does not measure Kerberos TGT/TGS lifetimes. Those come from Group Policy / registry (e.g. Maximum lifetime for user ticket). Filter intentionally matches no object; see docs/KERBEROS_TICKET_POLICY_LIMITS.md.

Remediation: Configure Kerberos policy via GPO or baseline tools.

References:

  • docs/KERBEROS_TICKET_POLICY_LIMITS.md

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-016: Kerberos renewal lifetime (LDAP placeholder — see ticket policy doc) Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: info | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Does not measure renewal lifetime policy. Filter matches no object. See docs/KERBEROS_TICKET_POLICY_LIMITS.md.

Remediation: Use GPO Kerberos policy settings.

References:

  • docs/KERBEROS_TICKET_POLICY_LIMITS.md

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-017: DC Lockout Threshold Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Account lockout disabled (lockoutThreshold=0) enables brute-force guessing.

Remediation: Enable account lockout with organizational thresholds.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-020: Min Password Length Fail

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 3

Minimum password length below 14 characters.

Remediation: Increase minPwdLength per baseline.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/
minPwdLength distinguishedName name CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
5 DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local sevenkingdoms DCONF-020 Min Password Length 1 Fail medium Minimum password length below 14 characters.

DCONF-021: Password Complexity Fail

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 3

Finding when DOMAIN_PASSWORD_COMPLEX (bit 1) is not set in pwdProperties.

Remediation: Enable password complexity in default domain policy.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/
distinguishedName name pwdProperties CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
DC=sevenkingdoms,DC=local sevenkingdoms 0 DCONF-021 Password Complexity 1 Fail medium Finding when DOMAIN_PASSWORD_COMPLEX (bit 1) is not set in pwdProperties.

DCONF-022: Password History Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 3 ms | Score: 0

Password history length below 24 (common STIG/CIS target).

Remediation: Increase pwdHistoryLength.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-023: Max Password Age Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

maxPwdAge indicates passwords never expire (weak).

Remediation: Set maximum password age per baseline.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-024: Min Password Age Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: low | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

minPwdAge of 0 allows immediate password changes (rotation abuse).

Remediation: Set minimum password age > 0 where appropriate.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/

No findings (Pass).

DCONF-025: Reversible Encryption Pass

Category: Domain_Configuration | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 4 ms | Score: 0

DOMAIN_PASSWORD_REVERSE_ENCRYPTED (0x80) enabled at domain — reversible encryption.

Remediation: Disable reversible encryption in domain policy unless legacy app requires it.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/

No findings (Pass).

GPO-ACL-001: SYSVOL Policies insecure ACLs Fail

Category: Group_Policy | Severity: high | Findings: 2 | Duration: 147 ms | Score: 8

Misconfiguration: GPO folders under SYSVOL\\Policies where Everyone or Authenticated Users have Modify/Write/FullControl (review and tighten).

Remediation: Remove excessive Modify/Write/FullControl from SYSVOL\\Policies subfolders; grant Domain Admins/Creator Owner and required DC groups only; validate GPO delegation.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/previous-versions/windows/it-pro/windows-server-2003/cc770880(v=ws.10)
  • Group Policy abuse (MITRE T1484.001)
GpoFolder IdentityReference FileSystemRights AccessControlType CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
\\sevenkingdoms.local\SYSVOL\sevenkingdoms.local\Policies\{31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9} NT AUTHORITY\Authenticated Users ReadAndExecute, Synchronize Allow GPO-ACL-001 SYSVOL Policies insecure ACLs 2 Fail high Misconfiguration: GPO folders under SYSVOL\\Policies where Everyone or Authenticated Users have Modify/Write/FullControl (review and tighten).

INFRA-033: Stale DNS Records Fail

Category: Infrastructure | Severity: medium | Findings: 8 | Duration: 10 ms | Score: 24

Infrastructure: Stale DNS Records — DNS, time, or core service misconfiguration. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Sync time; secure DNS; validate SRV records.

References:

  • Windows Server security baseline
  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/

KRB-001: Kerberoastable User Accounts Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Misconfiguration: Kerberoastable user accounts (SPN on user); overlaps conceptually with ACC-034 — use for Kerberos category reporting.

Remediation: Remove unnecessary SPNs from user accounts; prefer gMSA for services; enforce strong password policy for remaining SPN owners (same as ACC-034).

References:

  • Kerberoasting (MITRE T1558.003)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-002: AS-REP Roastable Accounts Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Misconfiguration: accounts with Kerberos pre-authentication disabled (AS-REP roastable).

Remediation: Enable Kerberos pre-authentication for affected accounts (or disable accounts that do not require interactive logon). Prefer group-based policies over per-account exceptions.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/security/threat-protection/auditing/event-4768
  • AS-REP roasting (MITRE ATT&CK)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-003: Unconstrained Delegation Computers Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 8 ms | Score: 0

Misconfiguration: non-DC computers with unconstrained delegation (TGT exposure risk).

Remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation or constrain to required services; treat as Tier0 adjacent.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-delegation-unconstrained
  • Unconstrained delegation (MITRE T1558.001)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-004: Unconstrained Delegation Users Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Misconfiguration: user accounts with unconstrained delegation enabled.

Remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation from user accounts unless business-critical; use constrained or resource-based delegation; treat as credential theft risk.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-delegation-unconstrained
  • Unconstrained delegation (MITRE T1558.001)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-005: Constrained Delegation Computers Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 7 ms | Score: 0

Exposure: computers with constrained delegation (msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo); validate SPN targets.

Remediation: Restrict msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo to required SPNs; remove stale delegation; prefer resource-based delegation where possible.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-delegation-constrained
  • Constrained delegation (MITRE T1558.001)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-006: Constrained Delegation Users Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 8 ms | Score: 0

Exposure: users with constrained delegation; review allowed SPNs.

Remediation: Tighten allowed SPNs; remove user-based constrained delegation if not required; monitor changes to msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-delegation-constrained
  • Constrained delegation (MITRE T1558.001)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-007: Protocol Transition S4U2Self Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Protocol transition / S4U (TRUSTED_TO_AUTH_FOR_DELEGATION) — constrained delegation abuse (S4U2Self/S4U2Proxy). Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Restrict msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo; remove unnecessary protocol transition; audit delegation.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-008: Resource-Based Constrained Delegation Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 15 ms | Score: 0

Resource-based constrained delegation (RBCD) — misconfiguration can allow impersonation to the resource. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Clear RBCD where not needed; scope principals; monitor changes to the attribute.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-009: DES-Only Kerberos Users Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Weak crypto: accounts allowed DES-only Kerberos (UAC bit); disable DES for the domain and clear per-account use of DES-only.

Remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos at domain level; clear the Use Kerberos DES encryption types only flag on accounts; migrate legacy apps off DES.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-encryption-types
  • Weak encryption (MITRE T1558)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-010: DES-Only Kerberos Computers Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Weak Kerberos encryption (DES / DES-capable) — tickets are easier to crack. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos domain-wide; clear USE_DES_KEY_ONLY on accounts; upgrade legacy apps.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-011: Accounts Supporting DES Encryption Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 14 ms | Score: 0

Weak Kerberos encryption (DES / DES-capable) — tickets are easier to crack. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos domain-wide; clear USE_DES_KEY_ONLY on accounts; upgrade legacy apps.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-012: Accounts Without Explicit Encryption Types Fail

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 15 | Duration: 19 ms | Score: 30

Kerberos: Accounts Without Explicit Encryption Types — ticket, encryption, or delegation weakness. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Kerberos hardening; delegation review.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

KRB-015: Accounts Not Delegated Privileged Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

adminCount=1 (adminSDHolder-protected object) — persistence of elevated ACLs or legacy protected state. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate protection is intended; remove inappropriate adminCount; align with tiering model.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

KRB-018: sMSA Accounts Legacy Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Kerberos: sMSA Accounts Legacy — ticket, encryption, or delegation weakness. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-019: Kerberoastable Accounts with Old Password Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove user SPNs where possible; use gMSA; enforce strong passwords for SPN owners.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-020: AS-REP Roastable SPN Double Risk Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 7 ms | Score: 0

AS-REP roasting: Kerberos pre-authentication disabled — offline cracking of account passwords without a TGT. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Enable pre-authentication on accounts; use strong passwords; avoid DONT_REQ_PREAUTH except rare documented exceptions.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove user SPNs where possible; use gMSA; enforce strong passwords for SPN owners.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

KRB-025: Computers with Protocol Transition Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Protocol transition / S4U (TRUSTED_TO_AUTH_FOR_DELEGATION) — constrained delegation abuse (S4U2Self/S4U2Proxy). Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Restrict msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo; remove unnecessary protocol transition; audit delegation.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-026: Trust Accounts TDOs Fail

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 2 | Duration: 8 ms | Score: 6

Trust or naming-context exposure: Trust Accounts TDOs — review for weak or unnecessary trust paths. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate trust type and SID filtering; remove stale trusts; monitor changes.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/manage-security-best-practices/understanding-trust-relationships
  • MITRE T1482

KRB-035: S4U2Proxy Abuse Detection Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 16 ms | Score: 0

Constrained delegation: service may impersonate users to specific SPNs — path to privilege escalation. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Minimize SPN targets; remove stale delegation; prefer RBCD for resource servers.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

Kerberos: Kerberos AES Not Enforced — ticket, encryption, or delegation weakness. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Kerberos hardening; delegation review.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

KRB-037: Unconstrained Delegation Computers Fail

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: critical | Findings: 1 | Duration: 7 ms | Score: 5

Unconstrained delegation on a non-DC computer — TGT theft and lateral movement to any service. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove TRUSTED_FOR_DELEGATION from member servers; use constrained or RBCD; isolate Tier0-adjacent hosts.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

KRB-041: Service Accounts with Unconstrained Delegation Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove user SPNs where possible; use gMSA; enforce strong passwords for SPN owners.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-042: Kerberos Pre Auth Not Required Computers Pass

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

AS-REP roasting: Kerberos pre-authentication disabled — offline cracking of account passwords without a TGT. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Enable pre-authentication on accounts; use strong passwords; avoid DONT_REQ_PREAUTH except rare documented exceptions.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

KRB-045: Protected Users Group Not Used Fail

Category: Kerberos_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 5 | Duration: 10 ms | Score: 20

adminCount=1 (adminSDHolder-protected object) — persistence of elevated ACLs or legacy protected state. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate protection is intended; remove inappropriate adminCount; align with tiering model.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

LDAP-002: LDAP Channel Binding Disabled Fail

Category: LDAP_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 1 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 3

LDAP: LDAP Channel Binding Disabled — unsigned or cleartext LDAP risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Require LDAP signing and TLS; disable simple bind.

References:

  • LDAP signing (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1040

LDAP-003: Anonymous LDAP Bind Allowed (SYSVOL GPO) Fail

Category: LDAP_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 1 | Duration: 30 ms | Score: 4

Same LDAP integrity signal as DCONF-009, driven from GptTmpl.inf.

Remediation: Require LDAP signing on DCs.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/
RuleId GptTmplPath RegistryKeyLine RawValue ParsedDword Detail CheckId CheckName FindingCount Result Severity Description
LDAPServerIntegrity_weak \\sevenkingdoms.local\SYSVOL\sevenkingdoms.local\Policies\{6AC1786C-016F-11D2-945F-00C04fB984F9}\Machine\Microsoft\Windows NT\SecEdit\GptTmpl.inf MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters\LDAPServerIntegrity 4,1 1 LDAP signing not required by GPO template. LDAP-003 Anonymous LDAP Bind Allowed (SYSVOL GPO) 1 Fail high Same LDAP integrity signal as DCONF-009, driven from GptTmpl.inf.

LDAP-006: LDAP Referral Chasing Issues Fail

LDAP: LDAP Referral Chasing Issues — unsigned or cleartext LDAP risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Require LDAP signing and TLS; disable simple bind.

References:

  • LDAP signing (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1040

PKI-001: AD-Integrated Certificate Authorities Pass

Category: PKI_Services | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

PKI (LDAP): AD-Integrated Certificate Authorities — template or ACL data may enable certificate-based abuse; review with PKI owners. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Review enrollment and template ACLs; disable weak or unused templates.

References:

  • AD CS / templates (LDAP view)
  • Microsoft PKI / AD CS hardening

No findings (Pass).

PKI (LDAP): CA Certificate Revocation Lists — template or ACL data may enable certificate-based abuse; review with PKI owners. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Review enrollment and template ACLs; disable weak or unused templates.

References:

  • AD CS / templates (LDAP view)
  • Microsoft PKI / AD CS hardening

No findings (Pass).

PRV-018: Admin Accounts with SPNs Kerberoasting Risk Pass

Category: Privileged_Access | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove user SPNs where possible; use gMSA; enforce strong passwords for SPN owners.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

PRV-030: Admin Accounts with DES Encryption Pass

Category: Privileged_Access | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Weak Kerberos encryption (DES / DES-capable) — tickets are easier to crack. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos domain-wide; clear USE_DES_KEY_ONLY on accounts; upgrade legacy apps.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

SECACCT-029: Claims Transformation Policies Pass

Category: Security_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Privileged access: Claims Transformation Policies — standing privilege or stale access. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: PIM; JIT; PAWS; minimal standing admin.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

Category: Security_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 10 ms | Score: 0

Privileged access: Claims Transformation Rules — standing privilege or stale access. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

SMB-001: SMBv1 Enabled (SYSVOL GPO) Pass

Category: SMB_Security | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 41 ms | Score: 0

Detects SMB1 enabled via GPO-deployed registry values in SecEdit templates.

Remediation: Disable SMBv1 via GPO and Server Manager features.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/storage/file-server/smb-v1-not-installed-by-default-in-windows

No findings (Pass).

SMB-002: SMB Signing Not Required (SYSVOL GPO) Pass

Category: SMB_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 33 ms | Score: 0

Client-side SMB signing requirement as expressed in GptTmpl.inf. Server-side coverage is in DC-033 / DCONF-011.

Remediation: Require SMB signing for workstations and servers.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/storage/file-server/smb-security

No findings (Pass).

Category: SMB_Security | Severity: medium | Findings: 2 | Duration: 13 ms | Score: 6

SMB: SMB Encryption Not Enabled — signing, encryption, or share exposure risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Enforce SMB signing; disable SMBv1; restrict shares.

SVC-001: Service Accounts SPN Inventory Error

Category: Service_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 26 ms | Score: 0

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove user SPNs where possible; use gMSA; enforce strong passwords for SPN owners.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

Error: LDAP query failed: An error occurred while enumerating through a collection: The (&(objectCategory=person)(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))) search filter is invalid..

SVC-002: Service Accounts with Password Never Expires Error

Category: Service_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove user SPNs where possible; use gMSA; enforce strong passwords for SPN owners.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

Error: LDAP query failed: An error occurred while enumerating through a collection: The (&(objectCategory=person)(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=65536)) search filter is invalid..

SVC-003: Service Accounts with Kerberos Pre-Auth Disabled Error

Category: Service_Accounts | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 7 ms | Score: 0

AS-REP roasting: Kerberos pre-authentication disabled — offline cracking of account passwords without a TGT. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Enable pre-authentication on accounts; use strong passwords; avoid DONT_REQ_PREAUTH except rare documented exceptions.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

Error: LDAP query failed: An error occurred while enumerating through a collection: The (&(objectCategory=person)(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=4194304)) search filter is invalid..

SVC-013: Service Accounts with KeyCredentialLink Error

Category: Service_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 7 ms | Score: 0

Shadow credentials: msDS-KeyCredentialLink can allow takeover without knowing the current password (WHfB / NTLM-less abuse). Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Restrict who can write KeyCredentialLink; audit keys on Tier0; remove unauthorized key credentials.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

Error: LDAP query failed: An error occurred while enumerating through a collection: The (&(objectCategory=person)(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))(msDS-KeyCredentialLink=*)) search filter is invalid..

SVC-014: Service Accounts with Script Path Error

Category: Service_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove user SPNs where possible; use gMSA; enforce strong passwords for SPN owners.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

Error: LDAP query failed: An error occurred while enumerating through a collection: The (&(objectCategory=person)(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))(scriptPath=*)) search filter is invalid..

SVC-020: Service Accounts with AccountExpires Error

Category: Service_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

Error: LDAP query failed: An error occurred while enumerating through a collection: The (&(objectCategory=person)(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))(accountExpires=*)) search filter is invalid..

SVC-021: SPN Accounts with Do not require Kerberos preauth Non-expiring password Error

Category: Service_Accounts | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

AS-REP roasting: Kerberos pre-authentication disabled — offline cracking of account passwords without a TGT. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Enable pre-authentication on accounts; use strong passwords; avoid DONT_REQ_PREAUTH except rare documented exceptions.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

Error: LDAP query failed: An error occurred while enumerating through a collection: The (&(objectCategory=person)(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=4194304)(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=65536)) search filter is invalid..

TMGMT-001: Trust Relationships Fail

Category: Trust_Management | Severity: medium | Findings: 2 | Duration: 8 ms | Score: 6

Trust or naming-context exposure: Trust Relationships — review for weak or unnecessary trust paths. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate trust type and SID filtering; remove stale trusts; monitor changes.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/manage-security-best-practices/understanding-trust-relationships
  • MITRE T1482

Remediation: Selective authentication; SID filtering; remove stale trusts.

Remediation: Validate trust type and SID filtering; remove stale trusts; monitor changes.

USR-001: Accounts Vulnerable to ASRepRoasting Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

AS-REP roasting: Kerberos pre-authentication disabled — offline cracking of account passwords without a TGT. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Enable pre-authentication on accounts; use strong passwords; avoid DONT_REQ_PREAUTH except rare documented exceptions.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

USR-002: Accounts Vulnerable to Kerberoasting Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Kerberoasting: user accounts with SPNs — ticket hashes can be cracked offline. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove user SPNs where possible; use gMSA; enforce strong passwords for SPN owners.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

USR-003: Accounts Vulnerable to Timeroasting Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 10 ms | Score: 0

Infrastructure: Accounts Vulnerable to Timeroasting — DNS, time, or core service misconfiguration. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Sync time; secure DNS; validate SRV records.

References:

  • Windows Server security baseline
  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/

No findings (Pass).

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 4 | Duration: 13 ms | Score: 16

adminCount=1 (adminSDHolder-protected object) — persistence of elevated ACLs or legacy protected state. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

USR-006: Accounts with DES Encryption Only Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Weak Kerberos encryption (DES / DES-capable) — tickets are easier to crack. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos domain-wide; clear USE_DES_KEY_ONLY on accounts; upgrade legacy apps.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

User accounts: Accounts with altSecurityIdentities — dormant accounts, weak settings, or excessive rights. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable unused users; MFA for admins; least privilege.

USR-014: Accounts with Never Expiring Passwords Fail

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 13 | Duration: 15 ms | Score: 40

Password never expires — long-lived passwords increase reuse and offline cracking risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove on sensitive accounts; enforce rotation; use PSO exceptions sparingly.

References:

  • Password policy (Microsoft Docs)
  • MITRE T1110

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

User accounts: Accounts with Reversible Password Encryption — dormant accounts, weak settings, or excessive rights. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable unused users; MFA for admins; least privilege.

USR-025: Native Administrator Account Recent Use Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 9 ms | Score: 0

User accounts: Native Administrator Account Recent Use — dormant accounts, weak settings, or excessive rights. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable unused users; MFA for admins; least privilege.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

USR-026: Group Managed Service Accounts gMSA Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 5 ms | Score: 0

Service account: Group Managed Service Accounts gMSA — interactive or over-privileged service logon risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Prefer gMSA; deny interactive logon; least privilege.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

USR-027: Standalone Managed Service Accounts sMSA Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Service account: Standalone Managed Service Accounts sMSA — interactive or over-privileged service logon risk. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Prefer gMSA; deny interactive logon; least privilege.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

USR-028: Protected Users Group Members Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

User accounts: Protected Users Group Members — dormant accounts, weak settings, or excessive rights. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Disable unused users; MFA for admins; least privilege.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

USR-029: Accounts with SID History Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: medium | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

SID History populated — migration artifact or malicious SID injection for privilege persistence. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove stale sIDHistory; investigate unexpected values; restrict writes to sIDHistory.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

USR-030: Accounts with KeyCredentialLink Shadow Credentials Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Shadow credentials: msDS-KeyCredentialLink can allow takeover without knowing the current password (WHfB / NTLM-less abuse). Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Restrict who can write KeyCredentialLink; audit keys on Tier0; remove unauthorized key credentials.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)

No findings (Pass).

USR-031: Accounts with Constrained Delegation Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Constrained delegation: service may impersonate users to specific SPNs — path to privilege escalation. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Minimize SPN targets; remove stale delegation; prefer RBCD for resource servers.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

USR-032: Accounts Trusted for Delegation Pass

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: critical | Findings: 0 | Duration: 6 ms | Score: 0

Unconstrained delegation on a user — impersonation and credential theft across services. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation from user accounts unless strictly required.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/kerberos/kerberos-authentication-overview
  • MITRE T1558 (Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets)

No findings (Pass).

USR-033: Recently Created Privileged Accounts Fail

Category: Users_Accounts | Severity: high | Findings: 5 | Duration: 10 ms | Score: 20

adminCount=1 (adminSDHolder-protected object) — persistence of elevated ACLs or legacy protected state. Risk: each object returned is a security-relevant finding until remediated or formally accepted.

Remediation: Validate protection is intended; remove inappropriate adminCount; align with tiering model.

References:

  • https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/security-best-practices/appendix-b--privileged-accounts-and-groups-in-active-directory
  • MITRE T1078 (Valid Accounts)
